target.name: ThinkOrbital
target.founder: Sebastian Asprella, Lee Rosen, Vojtech Holub

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ThinkOrbital is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation landscape for early-stage frontier tech in this sector remains constrained by limited comparable data; the broader Space & Aerospace universe includes 6 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though narrow-filter peer sets (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar valuation tier) lack sufficient depth for reliable median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, the stage-progression method provides the primary anchor. Using $8M total raised as a proxy for Seed valuation and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $20M. This method remains applicable where peer-based triangulation is not feasible due to insufficient same-stage comparables in the space sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M to $23M, centered on $20M. Material uncertainties include the absence of revenue data (precluding public-comp P/S anchoring), the thinness of Seed-stage comparable data in space and aerospace, and the nascent nature of ThinkOrbital's commercial trajectory. Any refinement would require either disclosure of financial metrics or identification of additional Seed-stage aerospace peers with known valuations.
